message:
fix(core): track metadata blocks in e2compr indirect writes + rollback on failure

The e2compr compressed-cluster write path previously only tracked data
blocks, silently dropping newly allocated indirect/double-indirect/triple-
indirect metadata blocks from the inode.blocks accounting.  This caused
i_blocks drift on any compressed write beyond direct block 11, and leaked
blocks when a multi-cluster write failed partway through.

Key changes:

- write_block_ptr now returns Vec<BlockNumber> of freshly allocated metadata
  blocks so callers can account for them in the inode block count.
- ensure_indirect_block and ensure_pointer_in_block return (BlockNumber,
  Vec<BlockNumber>) tuples, surfacing every new metadata allocation.
- Ext4CompressedClusterWrite gains a new_blocks field that captures all
  blocks (data + metadata) written during a cluster write.
- New helper ext4_checked_inode_blocks_after_cluster_rewrite computes the
  post-write i_blocks value with checked arithmetic, replacing the
  duplicated inline sector math in both compressed and uncompressed paths.
- collect_old_cluster_blocks now resolves indirect pointers via
  resolve_indirect_block instead of only scanning direct i_block entries,
  fixing block leaks on rewrite of indirect-region clusters.
- The top-level e2compr write loop is wrapped in a closure; on failure the
  newly allocated blocks in pending_new_blocks are freed, preventing block
  leaks on partial multi-cluster writes.
- E2E harness switches journal replay mode from SimulateOverlay to Apply.
- Two new unit tests validate indirect-cluster rewrite block accounting and
  multi-cluster failure rollback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
